blockingIssues.description: `fetchUniswapV3PoolSnapshots` queries `poolDayDatas` with `orderDirection: desc` and returns the array as-is. Any caller deriving candles or time-series from this helper receives reverse-chronological snapshots, which violates the feature contract for liquidity/volume snapshots as ordered history and risks invalid downstream series behavior.

The feature requirement says token detail/info surfaces should reflect live token pricing when DeFiLlama succeeds, but the token detail test still exercises only the seeded path and asserts `price_usd: 1` without mocking live pricing. That leaves `/onchain/networks/:network/tokens/:address` unproven even though the route was changed to call `fetchLiveSimpleTokenPrice`.

The new tests cover live-success responses for token info endpoints and live-derived token OHLCV, but they do not add a companion assertion proving graceful fallback to seeded token pricing/OHLCV when DeFiLlama or The Graph returns null/errors. The requested explicit live-vs-fallback distinction remains unproven.

The compare coverage summary test still declares a 15000 ms timeout. Although DeFiLlama stubbing reduced live-fetch cost, the feature requirement was to remove timeout-padding rather than continue depending on a raised timeout, so timeout masking was not fully removed in the reviewed implementation.

The commit only adjusts the compare suite to force seeded fallback and does not itself contain route-level or separate proof that live-expanded onchain network/dex/pool catalog behavior remains covered from current HEAD. That falls short of the feature's commit-attribution requirement.

Current HEAD now proves the live-expanded onchain catalog behavior, but `tests/compare-coingecko.test.ts` still retains `}, 15000);` on the endpoint coverage summary test. The feature explicitly required removing any remaining timeout-padding, so this acceptance criterion is still unmet.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that provider-only features may satisfy verification with automated tests when no route surface changes, instead of always requiring manual curl checks.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review `defillama-provider-module` found `.factory/skills/onchain-api-worker/SKILL.md` step 10 mandates API startup plus valid/invalid curl checks, while the provider-only handoff `2026-03-27T03-24-35-662Z__defillama-provider-module__950e6bc7-a164-4923-96ba-f9aa5d2e2bb2.json` only needed test/typecheck verification.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Allow commit-only sessions to skip the 'write failing tests first' requirement when the task explicitly says the implementation already exists and only verification/commit work remains.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Reviews for `wire-onchain-trades-ohlcv-prices-live` and related fix features note the worker had to record a procedural deviation solely because `.factory/skills/onchain-api-worker/SKILL.md` step 4 requires failing tests first even for commit-only assignments.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require fix-review handoffs to identify superseding implementation commits when current HEAD, not the original feature commit, is what resolves prior scrutiny failures.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review `wire-onchain-pool-endpoints-live` found handoff commit `6efb253` only changed provider fallback and a timeout bump, while current live-catalog dedupe/proof lived in later commits such as `3d5aa88e` and updated `tests/app.test.ts` coverage.

rejectedObservations.observation: Project research still documents older DeFiLlama pool endpoint details (`/pools` and `yields.llama.fi/pools`) that diverge from the fixed provider contract.
